question: Bitcoin Up or Down - March 21, 5:45AM-5:50AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Bitcoin price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Will the highest temperature in London be 9°C or below on March 24?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 24 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket's London highest temperature market for March 24 clusters tightly around 13–16°C, driven by the latest Met Office and ECMWF ensemble forecasts projecting maxima near 14°C under mild Atlantic westerlies. The 13°C lead (28%) reflects model consensus on persistent low cloud and stratiform rain limiting insolation, while 16°C (26%) odds price in potential high-pressure ridging to the south enabling southerly winds and clearer skies for greater daytime heating. Differentiating factors include jet stream positioning—northward for cooler, cloudy outcomes versus southward dips boosting temps—and frontal timing, with historical March norms at 11–12°C underscoring above-average potential; watch the next 12Z ECMWF run for shifts.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - March 22, 6AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket's London highest temperature market for March 24 clusters tightly around 13–16°C, driven by the latest Met Office and ECMWF ensemble forecasts projecting maxima near 14°C under mild Atlantic westerlies. The 13°C lead (28%) reflects model consensus on persistent low cloud and stratiform rain limiting insolation, while 16°C (26%) odds price in potential high-pressure ridging to the south enabling southerly winds and clearer skies for greater daytime heating. Differentiating factors include jet stream positioning—northward for cooler, cloudy outcomes versus southward dips boosting temps—and frontal timing, with historical March norms at 11–12°C underscoring above-average potential; watch the next 12Z ECMWF run for shifts.

question: Miami Open: Mirra Andreeva vs Marie Bouzkova
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Mirra Andreeva and Marie Bouzkova in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 21 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Mirra Andreeva' if Mirra Andreeva advances against Marie Bouzkova.

This market will resolve to 'Marie Bouzkova' if Marie Bouzkova advances against Mirra Andreeva.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mirra Andreeva's explosive recent form on hard courts anchors her 79.5% implied probability as the heavy favorite against Marie Bouzkova in the Miami Open round of 32. The 16-year-old Russian phenom cruised past Alexandra Eala in the first round, dropping just four games, building on her Indian Wells quarterfinal run and Dubai title earlier this year, where she's won 12 of 14 matches. Bouzkova, meanwhile, scraped by in her opener but enters with a 4-5 record over her last nine outings, including early exits in prior Sunshine Double events. No reported injuries for either, but Andreeva's superior serve hold rate (85%) and head-to-head edge in aggressive baseline play tilt the matchup dynamics sharply in her favor, reflecting trader consensus on her momentum amid favorable outdoor conditions.

description:

This market will resolve to "Fokina" if Alejandro Davidovich Fokina wins by 2 or more sets than Quentin Halys, based on the final completed score. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Halys."

eventMetadata.context_description: Davidovich Fokina's higher ranking (No. 25 vs. Halys at No. 84) and stronger hardcourt record anchor his 68% implied probability in this Miami Open matchup, reflecting trader consensus on his baseline power and consistency. He advanced past Nakashima in the first round (6-4, 3-6, 6-2), showing resilience despite a set lapse, while qualifier Halys edged Fucsovics (6-4, 7-5) but lacks the same firepower. Their lone head-to-head favors Fokina (2023 Basel win), with no reported injuries for either; however, Halys' big serve could test early if Fokina's return holds firm, though historical splits and recent momentum tilt odds toward the Spaniard amid neutral conditions.

question: Ethereum Up or Down - March 22, 6AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the ETH/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty in ensemble weather models, with ECMWF and GFS forecasts for Paris on March 24 projecting peak highs between 16°C and 20°C amid a mild Atlantic air mass advection, giving 18°C a slight 24% edge from Météo-France's localized guidance. Historical late-March norms hover at 12-13°C, but 2024's warmer baseline—driven by low Arctic sea ice—elevates implied probabilities, though frontal timing remains the key differentiator ahead of final 00Z runs.

question: Solana Up or Down - March 22, 6AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the SOL/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ensemble weather models from ECMWF, GFS, and Brazil's INMET converge on a Sao Paulo high of 29-31°C on March 24, anchoring the 37% implied probability for 30°C as traders' top outcome. question: XRP Up or Down - March 22, 6AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the XRP/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance XRP/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: Dogecoin Up or Down - March 22, 6AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the DOGE/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the DOGE/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/DOGE_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance DOGE/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Differentiation stems from 1–2°C model spread: warmer ECMWF ensembles versus cooler GFS, with uncertainty in boundary layer mixing and any late convective showers capping peaks below 13°C, reflected in balanced 25–26% probabilities for top outcomes.

question: HYPE Up or Down - March 22, 6AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the HYPE/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the HYPE/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/futures/HYPEUSDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance HYPE/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Uncertainty persists in timing of any late-day clearing.

question: BNB Up or Down - March 22, 6AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BNB/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BNB/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BNB_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BNB/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/wa/seatac/KSEA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment clusters around 52-53°F (38%) as the leading outcome, driven by the latest NOAA GFS and ECMWF ensemble forecasts projecting a daytime high of 52°F amid a cool maritime air mass over the Pacific Northwest. Seattle's position downwind of the Puget Sound introduces persistent marine stratus clouds, capping temperatures below seasonal norms—historical March 24 highs average 54°F but rarely exceed 60°F under similar synoptic setups with weak ridging aloft. Recent 12Z model runs refined this outlook, slightly boosting odds for 50-54°F ranges (combined ~92%) while diminishing extremes; a 00Z GFS update holds steady, with peak heating expected mid-afternoon before evening onshore flow reinforces moderation. Monitor NWS Seattle's hourly guidance for intraday shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for NYC's highest temperature on March 24 hinges on the razor-thin spread in NOAA's Global Forecast System (GFS) and ECMWF ensembles, with means clustering at 47-48°F amid a cool Canadian air mass and persistent upper-level trough. The 46-47°F bin (36.5%) edges out 48-49°F (32.0%) due to recent model runs emphasizing thicker cloud decks suppressing insolation, while slight boundary-layer mixing variations differentiate the bins—deeper mixing could nudge peaks toward 49°F. Historical March 24 highs average 51°F at Central Park, but this year's jet stream configuration favors 3-5°F anomalies below normal, with NWS updates through midday pivotal for final trader positioning.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts toward mid-to-upper 80s°F highs in Dallas on March 24, driven by the latest NOAA GFS and ECMWF ensemble forecasts converging on 85-89°F peaks amid a potent upper-ridge over the southern Plains advecting humid Gulf air northward. This setup promises ample insolation and weak mixing heights, favoring strong daytime heating after morning lows near 65°F. Close odds reflect model spreads: drier soils and reduced cloudiness in recent 12Z runs boost 88-89°F (25%) over 84-85°F (22.5%), while lingering boundary-layer moisture could cap peaks at 86-87°F (21.5%). Historical March 24 averages hover at 72°F, underscoring the anomalous warmth from persistent ridging, though afternoon cumulus remains a key uncertainty differentiating outcomes.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Wellington Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/nz/wellington/NZWN.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus clusters tightly around 19–22°C for Wellington's March 24 high, driven by MetService's latest forecast predicting a maximum near 20°C under partly cloudy skies with light northerlies and isolated showers limiting peak heating. This reflects ensemble model agreement from ECMWF and GFS, showing a weak frontal boundary introducing uncertainty—northerly flow favors 21–22°C via warm advection, while increased cloud cover or southerly shifts could cap at 18–19°C. Historical March 24 averages hover at 20°C, but current sea surface temperatures 1°C above normal slightly boost upside risk, explaining the 9% tail for 23°C+ amid model spread. Key watch: afternoon forecast refinements.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors 32°C (35%) as Lucknow's highest temperature on March 24, propelled by the Indian Meteorological Department's (IMD) latest forecast capping daytime highs at 32-33°C amid seasonal pre-monsoon warming and lingering effects from a recent western disturbance. Observations from nearby stations yesterday hit 33.4°C, but cloud cover and moderate winds are suppressing extremes, bolstering odds for 31°C or below (24.5%) and 33°C (23.5%). Ensemble models like GFS and ECMWF show low confidence in 41°C+ outliers (19%), citing ample soil moisture and neutral jet stream positioning that historically limit March spikes beyond 35°C in Uttar Pradesh. Final resolution hinges on IMD's verified airport readings.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Munich Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 24 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Munich Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/de/munich/EDDM.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment clusters tightly around 13–16°C for Munich's March 24 high temperature, driven by divergent short-range forecasts from ECMWF and GFS ensembles averaging 14°C amid a mild Atlantic ridge boosting geopotential heights over Central Europe. Leading models show ECMWF leaning cooler at 13–14°C due to anticipated mid-level clouds, while GFS implies warmer 16°C+ potential with clearer skies and southerly flow. Historical March 24 highs average 11–12°C at Munich Airport, but current +2–3°C anomalies from weakened polar vortex differentiate outcomes; cloud cover timing remains the key uncertainty, with DWD's next update pivotal for resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders heavily favor a 19°C high temperature in Tel Aviv on March 24 at 32% implied probability, driven by ECMWF and GFS ensemble consensus forecasting peaks of 18-20°C amid mild late-winter Mediterranean conditions with average March highs historically at 19°C. High uncertainty stems from model divergences on sea breeze strength and cloud cover remnants from recent frontal systems, potentially cooling outcomes to 16-17°C (35% combined) via northerly winds or allowing 20-21°C (32%) with southerly flow advection. Israeli Meteorological Service updates emphasize stable jet stream positioning limiting extremes, as short-range forecasts refine in the next day.
